conceptClusters.title: Retrieved lists content models and persistent labels
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves list relationships for one-dimensional sequences, exact parent-child content models, and persistent visible/programmatic naming before choosing a table.
Learner repairs changed list grouping, invalid table descendants, and missing table or control context without copying earlier work.

conceptClusters.title: Genuine multidimensional data and table identification
exitEvidence: Learner selects table only when row and column relationships are necessary, rejects page layout and one-dimensional content tables, and chooses lists, prose, definition lists, cards, or CSS layout when appropriate.

Learner gives every data table concise visible identification, orientation help only when complexity warrants it, and verifies scan, table-navigation, linearized, copied, zoomed, and narrow-width use.

conceptClusters.title: Caption columns row groups rows and cell grid
exitEvidence: Learner builds conforming ordered table children, caption, optional column groups, one head, body groups, optional foot, rows, header cells, and data cells from actual data relationships.
Learner verifies parsed DOM, row and column counts, section membership, cell coordinates, text, empty-cell meaning, linear order, and complete non-overlapping grid after rows or columns change.

conceptClusters.title: Necessary row and column spans
exitEvidence: Learner computes rowspan and colspan from real grouped headers or repeated data relationships, uses positive valid spans only where they reduce false repetition, and rejects spans used merely for appearance.
Learner verifies every spanned origin and covered coordinate, group boundary, table width, no overlap or hole, changed row and column insertion, and sensible linearized reading.

conceptClusters.title: Header cells scopes and explicit associations
exitEvidence: Learner uses th for actual headers, simple row and column scope when useful, rowgroup and colgroup only with matching groups and spans, and explicit unique id/headers lists only when genuine multi-level or irregular relationships need them.

Learner derives the exact ordered header context for every changed data cell, rejects styled td and guessed scope, preserves associations after row, column, group, and span changes, and verifies responsive table navigation without duplicating hidden labels that contradict semantics.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An evidence map explains how an unseen service dataset becomes a list or genuine table, conforming grid, necessary spans, and exact header context.
activityDesigns.startingState: A content brief, candidate lists and layouts, table DOM traces, grid coordinates, span diagrams, header associations, linearized text, and narrow-width traces are provided without decisions.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner predicts and explains all seven concepts under changed rows, columns, spans, and relationships before guided authoring.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts committed prediction with one observable content, DOM, geometry, header, or responsive fact and never supplies finished table.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one transition at a time from data relationship to structure, geometry, context, or alternative.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires revised prediction and smaller changed trace before model continues.

interactions.title: Retrieve one-dimensional lists
interactions.learnerAction: Predict ordered, unordered, and term-description structures for changed one-dimensional service content before considering a grid.

evidence.observable: Sequence, grouping, and term relationships use list semantics without artificial columns.
evidence.changedCase: Ranked steps become unordered resources.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any repeated items belong in table rows.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify one-dimensional relationship first.

interactions.title: Retrieve table content models
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect permitted order and parents for caption, colgroup, thead, tbody, tfoot, tr, th, and td in valid and parser-repaired source.
interactions.artifactChange: A table parent-child map is saved.

evidence.observable: Parsed DOM and author conformance follow exact table contexts and ordering.
evidence.changedCase: Caption and row move to invalid parents.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual indentation proves table validity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply exact permitted context and order.

interactions.title: Retrieve persistent naming
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect visible heading, caption, nearby explanation, control labels, accessible table name, and descriptions without conflating their roles.
interactions.artifactChange: A table naming-role map is saved.

evidence.observable: Table topic and any interactive control purpose remain visible and programmatically available.
evidence.changedCase: Nearby heading changes while caption remains.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Proximity or placeholder-like first row names table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate page heading, caption, explanation, and control label.

interactions.title: Predict genuine table purpose
interactions.learnerAction: Choose table, list, definition list, prose, cards, or CSS layout for changed schedules, rankings, contact cards, label-value facts, and page regions.

evidence.observable: Table is used only when intersecting row and column relationships matter.
evidence.changedCase: Two-dimensional schedule becomes one event list.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rows and columns desired visually justify table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether two axes are needed for meaning.

interactions.title: Inspect table grid formation
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect caption, columns, row groups, rows, headers, data cells, empty cells, coordinates, text, and complete grid in a changed schedule.
interactions.artifactChange: A parsed grid map is saved.

evidence.observable: Every cell has correct section, row, column, dimensions, text, and non-overlapping coordinate.
evidence.changedCase: A row and column are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Matching source cell counts prove grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Form grid from parsed rows and cell coordinates.

interactions.title: Predict span geometry
interactions.learnerAction: Predict origins, covered coordinates, row and column groups, grid width, holes, and overlaps for changed rowspan and colspan values.
interactions.artifactChange: A span geometry prediction is committed.

evidence.observable: Spans cover exactly intended coordinates within real groups without overlap or holes.
evidence.changedCase: A subgroup column and data row are added.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged visual rectangle proves valid span.
interactions.correctionRoute: Calculate every coordinate from origin and extent.

interactions.title: Predict cell header context
interactions.learnerAction: Predict ordered row, column, rowgroup, colgroup, and explicit header context for simple, grouped, and irregular changed cells.

evidence.observable: Each data cell receives exact actual headers and no unrelated styled cells.
evidence.changedCase: Grouped header becomes irregular and one ID changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold cells or nearest header provide context automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive associations from geometry and explicit references.

interactions.title: Explain complete table model
interactions.learnerAction: Explain purpose, alternative, caption, content models, grid, spans, header context, linearization, zoom, narrow-width behavior, and changed evidence as one system.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete table evidence map becomes model checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every claim links data meaning to DOM, geometry, context, access, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Schedule becomes grouped comparison with extra axis.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Table-element inventory substitutes for relationship model.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild weakest claim from data axes to cell context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A clinic service schedule accumulates genuine tabular purpose, visible identification, conforming row groups, complete grid, necessary spans, exact header associations, and usable narrow-width navigation.
activityDesigns.startingState: A verified clinic dataset, content alternatives, schedule axes, grouping facts, responsive requirements, deterministic mutations, and minimal valid document are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: Saved schedule passes all seven contracts, changed rows and columns, table navigation, linearization, copy, zoom, narrow-width use, and reload persistence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names first contradicted content, DOM, grid, span, header, or access observable and withholds final markup.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints progress from relationship classification to one evidence layer to smaller parallel cell or grid.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires diagnosis, bounded repair, and changed clinic case before original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Classify non-tabular clinic content
interactions.learnerAction: Classify announcements, service steps, eligibility terms, contacts, and clinic regions as ordered lists, unordered lists, definition lists, prose, or layout before table work.

evidence.observable: One-dimensional and term relationships retain correct semantics.
evidence.changedCase: Eligibility facts become ordered process steps.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All repeated service content becomes rows.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify content relationship before visual layout.

interactions.title: Inspect clinic table contexts
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect allowed table-child order, row-group parents, row children, cell content, caption position, and parser repairs before coding.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic table content constraints are saved.

evidence.observable: Every planned relationship is conforming in parsed DOM.
evidence.changedCase: Foot row and caption move in source.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browser rendering means source relationships are valid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply permitted contexts and child order.

interactions.title: Code persistent schedule identification
interactions.learnerAction: Code visible page heading, concise table caption, bounded orientation text for complexity, and labels for any schedule controls without duplicated or hidden contradictions.
interactions.artifactChange: Schedule naming and orientation are committed.

evidence.observable: Table topic and interactive control purposes remain visible, distinct, and programmatic.
evidence.changedCase: Page contains a second related table.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nearby heading alone identifies every table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Provide table-specific caption and control labels.

interactions.title: Defend clinic schedule as table
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether service, day, location, and time intersections require a table and reject layout-table and card-grid alternatives that lose comparison.

evidence.observable: Two-dimensional lookup and comparison require row-column intersections.
evidence.changedCase: Dataset collapses to one day's ordered services.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Grid-like visual design justifies table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test row and column relationships under linearization.

interactions.title: Inspect scan and lookup tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect how users find one service time, compare days, scan closures, copy one row, and understand table topic through visual and table-navigation paths.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic table task inventory is saved.

evidence.observable: Chosen representation supports every bounded lookup and comparison task.
evidence.changedCase: Primary task changes from lookup to chronological reading.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Full dataset presence proves usable representation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match representation to real information tasks.

interactions.title: Test table identification and separation
interactions.learnerAction: Navigate between two clinic tables, inspect captions and nearby explanations, linearize, copy, and verify each dataset remains separately identifiable.

evidence.observable: Each table has unique visible topic and bounded orientation without merging datasets.
evidence.changedCase: Second table receives similar title and new rows.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One shared page heading names both tables.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify each table at its own boundary.

interactions.title: Explain clinic representation choice
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why each content block uses table, list, prose, definition list, cards, or CSS layout from actual meaning and changed tasks.

evidence.observable: Every choice links relationship, task, alternative, access evidence, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Schedule becomes single-axis event feed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Element preference substitutes for task evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite one choice from relationship to user task.

interactions.title: Predict clinic table child order
interactions.learnerAction: Predict caption, colgroup, thead, tbody groups, tfoot, rows, and cells from clinic dataset roles before coding.

evidence.observable: Ordered children and section roles match data structure.
evidence.changedCase: Summary row becomes body data and new body group appears.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual top and bottom position defines head and foot.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify structural data role and required order.

interactions.title: Code caption and row groups
interactions.learnerAction: Code caption first, optional columns only when useful, one header group, separate body groups for actual clinic categories, and justified footer summary.

evidence.observable: Parsed DOM order and section membership match dataset relationships.
evidence.changedCase: One clinic category is removed and footer becomes body row.

interactions.feedbackTarget: thead and tfoot are chosen only for bold styling.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive sections from repeated data roles.

interactions.title: Code rows headers and data cells
interactions.learnerAction: Code one row per record with th only for actual headers, td for data, explicit empty-cell meaning, and no cells used for spacing.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic rows and cells are committed.

evidence.observable: Cell roles, text, coordinates, and empty meanings match source data.
evidence.changedCase: One service gains unknown rather than empty value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold text makes th and blank td creates spacing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify header/data role and missing-data meaning.

interactions.title: Inspect parsed clinic grid
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect section, row index, column index, cell role, text, width, height, and occupancy for every parsed cell.

evidence.observable: Grid is fully covered without overlap and every coordinate has intended cell.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column are inserted in middle.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Same number of source cells per row proves grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed coordinates and occupancy.

interactions.title: Test row and column insertion
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, remove, and reorder clinic rows and columns while updating caption facts, section membership, cells, empty values, and grid coverage.

evidence.observable: DOM structure, text, coordinates, dimensions, and complete grid remain accurate.
evidence.changedCase: Two columns merge into one different metric.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered alignment proves data survived mutation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild data schema and every row coordinate.

interactions.title: Test linearized clinic reading
interactions.learnerAction: Remove table styling, linearize source, copy rows, print, and inspect whether caption, sections, row order, cell text, and missing-data labels remain intelligible.
interactions.artifactChange: Linearization and copy evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Text and structural order retain complete data without visual borders.
evidence.changedCase: Long clinic names and notes double.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Styled borders are required for data meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve semantic structure and explicit text.

interactions.title: Explain clinic grid structure
interactions.learnerAction: Explain every table child, section, row, cell role, empty state, coordinate, order, and changed-grid repair from data relationships.

evidence.observable: Each claim links dataset role to DOM, coordinate, text, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Body group becomes separate table.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Element inventory replaces grid evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite one row from schema through coordinates.

interactions.title: Predict grouped header spans
interactions.learnerAction: Predict colspan origins and covered columns for service-category headers above changed individual service columns.

evidence.observable: Each group header covers exactly its actual contiguous columns.
evidence.changedCase: Service moves between groups and new column appears.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Centered visual header defines group width.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count actual covered grid columns.

interactions.title: Code necessary column spans
interactions.learnerAction: Code valid positive colspan values only for real grouped headers and keep ungrouped headers at one column.

evidence.observable: Origins, extents, group boundaries, grid width, and cells align without overlap or hole.
evidence.changedCase: A group splits into two groups.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Colspan is used to center appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive extent from data group membership.

interactions.title: Predict row-group spans
interactions.learnerAction: Predict rowspan origins and covered rows when one location header applies to multiple changed service rows within a real body group.

evidence.observable: Header covers intended contiguous rows without crossing unrelated body group.
evidence.changedCase: Service row moves to another location group.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated labels should always be merged visually.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test actual grouped relationship and row boundaries.

interactions.title: Code necessary row spans
interactions.learnerAction: Code justified rowspan values and leave repeated data separate when merging would hide independent records or complicate change.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic row spans and refusals are committed.

evidence.observable: Every covered row shares real group meaning and remains within table model.
evidence.changedCase: Rows reorder and repeated location no longer contiguous.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Removing repeated visual text always improves table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve record independence unless group is real.

interactions.title: Inspect span occupancy
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect every spanned origin, width, height, covered coordinate, section boundary, table width, hole, overlap, and downstream cell position.

evidence.observable: Complete grid has no overlap, hole, cross-group error, or shifted cell.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column are inserted inside spans.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browser-rendered merged boxes prove valid geometry.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map every coordinate and collision explicitly.

interactions.title: Test span mutation and linearization
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, delete, and move grouped rows and columns, recalculate spans, linearize, copy, and verify group meaning and complete grid.

evidence.observable: Spans follow changed data groups and text remains understandable outside ideal rendering.
evidence.changedCase: Both group axes change simultaneously.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Original fixed span values remain valid after changes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute from current schema, never patch visually.

interactions.title: Explain clinic span decisions
interactions.learnerAction: Explain every used and refused rowspan and colspan from grouping, coordinate, linearization, change cost, and header-context evidence.

evidence.observable: Each span claim links real relationship to coordinates, access, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Stakeholder requests decorative merged cells.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged appearance substitutes for group meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite one span from data group through coordinates.

interactions.title: Predict simple header contexts
interactions.learnerAction: Predict column and row header context for each simple service-time data cell before adding explicit scope.

evidence.observable: Each data cell receives exact actual row and column headers from geometry.
evidence.changedCase: Row-header column moves and column order changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Top row and first column are headers by position alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify actual header meaning and cell geometry.

interactions.title: Code actual header cells and simple scope
interactions.learnerAction: Code th only for real headers and add row or col scope when it clarifies direction, leaving data cells as td regardless of bold styling.
interactions.artifactChange: Simple clinic header associations are committed.

evidence.observable: Cell role, scope, geometry, and derived context agree.
evidence.changedCase: A previously descriptive cell becomes data.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold td or th everywhere improves accessibility.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify whether cell labels other cells.

interactions.title: Code grouped header scopes
interactions.learnerAction: Use colgroup and rowgroup scope only for real header groups with matching spans and row or column group boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: Grouped clinic header associations are committed.

evidence.observable: Group headers apply exactly to intended member headers and data cells.
evidence.changedCase: Group splits and one member moves.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any spanning header uses group scope.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify real group, span, and boundary together.

interactions.title: Predict explicit association need
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether simple scope remains sufficient or irregular multi-level relationships require unique header IDs and ordered headers references.

evidence.observable: Explicit references are reserved for genuine complexity and cover all required headers.
evidence.changedCase: Regular grouped table becomes irregular.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every cell should use headers attributes for safety.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use simplest verified association model.

interactions.title: Code explicit irregular associations
interactions.learnerAction: Assign stable unique IDs to actual headers and exact space-separated headers references to affected cells without dangling, duplicate, cyclic, or unrelated tokens.
interactions.artifactChange: Explicit clinic associations are committed where needed.

evidence.observable: Every token resolves to intended th and yields complete cell context.
evidence.changedCase: Header ID changes and irregular column is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Token presence proves accurate association.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve every reference and compare expected context.

interactions.title: Inspect every cell context
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect each data cell's text, coordinate, row, column, group, implicit or scoped headers, explicit references, and final ordered header context.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete clinic header-context matrix is saved.

evidence.observable: Every data value can be understood from exact associated headers without visual position.
evidence.changedCase: Rows, columns, groups, spans, and IDs mutate together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Spot-checking first cell proves whole table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify context for every cell and changed case.

interactions.title: Test table navigation and copy
interactions.learnerAction: Navigate row and column by table commands, inspect announced context, copy changed rows, and verify headers remain accurate after sorting-like reordering without claiming interactive sorting.
interactions.artifactChange: Table-navigation and copy evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Every navigated and copied value retains correct row, column, and group context.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column order reverse.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual zebra stripes provide context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use programmatic header relationships and explicit text.

interactions.title: Test narrow two-dimensional use
interactions.learnerAction: Zoom, narrow, increase text, use keyboard and touch, follow bounded horizontal table scrolling where necessary, and verify caption, full cells, focus, context, and page-wide reflow.

evidence.observable: Necessary grid scroll stays local, all content and context remain available, and page does not clip.
evidence.changedCase: Longest header and cell text double.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Stacking cells with generated labels automatically preserves semantics.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve real table structure and verify local navigation.

interactions.title: Test styles and hidden-label removal
interactions.learnerAction: Remove author styles, color, sticky behavior, duplicated pseudo-labels, and visual-only cues; verify caption, cell roles, header contexts, focus, and full grid remain correct.

evidence.observable: Meaning and navigation rely on HTML relationships rather than presentation-generated text.
evidence.changedCase: All decorative cues disappear together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS-generated labels repair missing headers.
interactions.correctionRoute: Fix source semantics, then layer presentation.

interactions.title: Explain clinic header system
interactions.learnerAction: Explain th and td roles, simple scope, group scope, explicit references, every data-cell context, responsive behavior, and changed-association repair.

evidence.observable: Each claim links data relationship to geometry, association, navigation, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Regular schedule becomes irregular multi-level comparison.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Scope and headers token list substitutes for context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite one cell from coordinates through announced headers.

interactions.title: Run complete clinic schedule regression
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, change representation task, caption, child order, rows, columns, groups, empty values, spans, cell roles, scopes, IDs, references, zoom, and width; retest all seven contracts.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic schedule and full regression suite become workshop checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Saved meaning, DOM, grid, geometry, context, tasks, and access survive every mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel clinic dataset reverses all axes and groups.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Initial desktop table match is completion.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace first broken relationship through cell context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A partially scaffolded transit timetable becomes a distinct, accessible data table through fading support and changed-grid checks.
activityDesigns.startingState: Different transit dataset, mixed list and layout candidates, incomplete caption, broken table sections, partial spans, missing header associations, and narrow-width requirements are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner completes all seven concepts and transfers them to changed routes with only concept prompts.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names failed evidence after commitment and never supplies final grid, span, or header markup.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints fade from relationship category to coordinate or context question without finished code.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires smaller parallel correction and changed timetable case before retry.

interactions.title: Complete timetable prerequisites
interactions.learnerAction: Complete lists for one-dimensional route notes, valid table parent-child relationships, and persistent timetable and filter identification.

evidence.observable: List meaning, parsed DOM, caption, descriptions, and control labels pass.
evidence.changedCase: Route notes reorder, caption changes, and row moves parent.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All repeated content becomes table rows.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair content relationship, model, and name separately.

interactions.title: Complete timetable representation
interactions.learnerAction: Complete table, list, prose, and CSS-layout choices for route times, stop sequences, service alerts, fares, and page regions from actual information tasks.

evidence.observable: Only route-stop-time intersections use table semantics.
evidence.changedCase: One route becomes chronological stop list.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Grid appearance justifies table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether two axes are required for meaning.

interactions.title: Test timetable identification and tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Navigate multiple tables, inspect captions, look up one departure, compare routes, copy a row, linearize, zoom, and narrow.

evidence.observable: Each table remains identifiable and supports bounded lookup and comparison.
evidence.changedCase: Primary task changes to single-route reading.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Dataset visibility proves representation works.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match representation and identification to tasks.

interactions.title: Code timetable grid structure
interactions.learnerAction: Code caption, justified column and row groups, rows, actual header cells, data cells, and explicit missing-value text from route data.

evidence.observable: Ordered DOM, section roles, coordinates, cell text, and complete grid pass.
evidence.changedCase: Route row and stop column are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equal source cell counts prove structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Build parsed grid from current data schema.

interactions.title: Test timetable grid mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Remove, insert, reorder, and regroup routes and stops; inspect DOM, coordinates, empty values, linear order, copy output, and full occupancy.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit grid mutation evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Every coordinate remains covered once with intended data and role.
evidence.changedCase: Two stop columns merge into one zone.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered alignment proves data survived.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild schema and map every coordinate.

interactions.title: Code transit group spans
interactions.learnerAction: Code only necessary route-zone and day-group rowspans and colspans from actual contiguous groups, refusing decorative merges.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit spans and refusals are committed.

evidence.observable: Origins, extents, boundaries, width, and occupancy match real groups.
evidence.changedCase: Route moves zone and weekday group splits.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged visual labels always reduce repetition safely.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive each extent from current group membership.

interactions.title: Test timetable span changes
interactions.learnerAction: Insert and delete rows and columns inside and beside spans, recalculate geometry, linearize, copy, and inspect holes, overlaps, cross-group errors, and shifted cells.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit span mutation evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Complete grid and group meaning survive all geometry changes.
evidence.changedCase: Both axes regroup simultaneously.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Original fixed span values remain valid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute every span and downstream coordinate.

interactions.title: Code timetable header contexts
interactions.learnerAction: Code actual th and td roles, simple row and column scope, justified group scope, and explicit ID/headers only for irregular changed relationships.

evidence.observable: Every data cell receives exact route, stop, day, and group headers.
evidence.changedCase: Regular group becomes irregular multi-level context.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold cells and nearest headers provide context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use simplest association that verifies every cell.

interactions.title: Test timetable association mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder routes and stops, change spans and groups, rename header IDs, mutate headers references, and inspect every data-cell context.

evidence.observable: Every reference resolves and every cell retains complete relevant context only.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column group structures reverse.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Spot-checking first row proves associations.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify every cell after each geometry change.

interactions.title: Test responsive timetable use
interactions.learnerAction: Navigate table by keyboard and touch, zoom, narrow, increase text, use local two-dimensional scroll, inspect headers, copy rows, and remove generated labels and styles.

evidence.observable: Full grid, caption, focus, text, and programmatic context remain usable without page-wide clipping.
evidence.changedCase: Longest stop and route labels double.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Stacked cells with CSS labels preserve table automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve HTML relationships and test local scroll.

interactions.title: Run transit mutation suite
interactions.learnerAction: Change content representation, caption, DOM order, rows, columns, groups, empty values, spans, cell roles, scopes, IDs, references, zoom, width, save, and reload.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven-concept transit regression suite is stored.

evidence.observable: All meaning, grid, geometry, context, and access contracts survive mutations and reload.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel timetable reverses all axes and groups.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Original desktop table is transfer evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Isolate first broken relationship and preserve others.

interactions.title: Explain timetable transfer
interactions.learnerAction: Explain all seven concepts, alternatives, DOM, grid, spans, cell contexts, responsive use, corrections, and one parallel transfer without workshop notes.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit timetable and defense become faded checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every claim links data relationship to grid, context, access, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Timetable becomes fare-zone comparison.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Table tag inventory replaces transfer evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild weakest claim from axes through cell context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A broken public-data table gains minimal causal repairs and incident report spanning alternative content, DOM, grid, spans, header context, and narrow-width use.
activityDesigns.startingState: Page uses layout table, flattened lists, invalid table children, missing caption, holes and overlaps, decorative spans, styled td headers, wrong scope, dangling headers references, and clipped responsive wrapper.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every fault has earliest cause, direct evidence, minimal repair, preserved behavior, and changed parallel incident.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies contradicted content, DOM, coordinate, association, or access evidence without repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints narrow incident to one concept and smaller cell trace.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires causal classification, minimal repair, regression evidence, and changed fault.

interactions.title: Predict prerequisite failures
interactions.learnerAction: Predict consequences of flattened lists, invalid table children, missing caption, and unlabelled table controls before inspection.

evidence.observable: Predictions map to lost sequence, parser DOM, table identity, and control purpose.
evidence.changedCase: Faults move into second table and filter.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All failures are generic table accessibility issues.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify content, model, and naming separately.

interactions.title: Repair table-purpose misuse
interactions.learnerAction: Replace layout tables and one-dimensional tables with CSS layout, lists, definition lists, or prose while preserving genuine multidimensional datasets and captions.

evidence.observable: Each representation supports actual relationship and task without confusing table navigation.
evidence.changedCase: One dataset collapses to single axis.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding presentation role repairs layout table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose representation from data relationships.

interactions.title: Repair broken grid structure
interactions.learnerAction: Repair child order, row-group misuse, header/data roles, missing cells, spacing cells, implicit missing values, holes, overlaps, and parser-created relationships.

evidence.observable: Parsed DOM, section roles, text, coordinates, and full occupancy pass.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column are inserted after repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equal source cells or visual alignment prove grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild parsed grid from actual schema.

interactions.title: Repair span geometry faults
interactions.learnerAction: Repair zero, invalid, decorative, stale, cross-group, overlapping, hole-producing, and downstream-shifting rowspans and colspans.
interactions.artifactChange: Span faults receive minimal repairs or removal.

evidence.observable: Origins, extents, boundaries, width, and occupancy match real groups.
evidence.changedCase: Both axes gain members after repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged visual rectangles validate spans.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recalculate every coordinate and group boundary.

interactions.title: Repair header association faults
interactions.learnerAction: Repair styled td headers, th used as data, guessed scope, mismatched group scope and spans, duplicate IDs, dangling or unrelated headers tokens, and incomplete contexts.

evidence.observable: Every data cell has exact complete relevant context using simplest valid mechanism.
evidence.changedCase: Regular header system becomes irregular.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding scope or headers everywhere repairs context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive context from actual geometry and relationships.

interactions.title: Inspect repaired table layers
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect representation, caption, DOM, row groups, coordinates, occupancy, spans, IDs, references, per-cell headers, linear text, copy, focus, and narrow-width behavior.

evidence.observable: All seven layers agree and no repair masks another fault.
evidence.changedCase: Rows, columns, groups, and labels change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered screenshot proves repairs.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare every semantic, geometric, and access layer.

interactions.title: Test changed table incident
interactions.learnerAction: Insert and reorder rows and columns, regroup spans, rename IDs, remove styles and pseudo-labels, zoom, narrow, navigate, copy, save, reload, and retest every cell.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete changed incident suite is stored.

evidence.observable: Meaning, grid, spans, associations, navigation, and persistence survive all changes.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel table relocates every fault.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One corrected desktop view passes clinic.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace first regression from data axes to cell context.

interactions.title: Write table incident report
interactions.learnerAction: Explain each requirement, cause, contradicted evidence, minimal repair, preserved invariant, changed case, responsive result, and every-cell header result across all seven concepts.
interactions.artifactChange: Repaired table and causal report become clinic checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Report demonstrates relationship and geometry diagnosis rather than tag substitution.
evidence.changedCase: Second dataset repeats mechanisms in new axes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Report lists changed elements without cell evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite weakest incident from data relationship to context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently built community sports standings table supports genuine multidimensional comparison, exact grid geometry, necessary grouped spans, per-cell header context, and usable narrow-width navigation.
activityDesigns.startingState: Unseen league dataset, ranking tasks, alternative-content candidates, grouping facts, tie and missing-data rules, responsive requirements, deterministic mutations, and empty workspace are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: Saved standings pass all seven contracts, changed teams and metrics, table navigation, copy, linearization, zoom, local scrolling, persistence, and stakeholder defense without copied structure.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports only unmet content, DOM, geometry, context, or access observable and supplies no final table code.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints available only on request and point to one layer or smaller parallel standings cell.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires independent diagnosis, minimal repair, and changed league case before checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Build standings prerequisites
interactions.learnerAction: Build lists for one-dimensional rules, valid table parent-child structure, visible standings caption and orientation, and labels for any filters.

evidence.observable: List semantics, parsed DOM, table identity, explanation, and control labels pass.
evidence.changedCase: Rules reorder, second table appears, and row moves parent.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All repeated sports content belongs in table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair content, model, and naming separately.

interactions.title: Choose standings representation
interactions.learnerAction: Build genuine team-by-metric standings as table while routing ranking notes, tie rules, contacts, and page layout to lists, prose, or CSS.

evidence.observable: Table is reserved for row-column intersections needed for lookup and comparison.
evidence.changedCase: Dataset becomes one ordered ranking with no metrics.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Grid appearance determines table use.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether both axes are required for meaning.

interactions.title: Test standings information tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Identify table, find one team's score, compare metrics, inspect tie, copy a row, linearize, navigate between tables, zoom, and narrow.

evidence.observable: Representation and identification support every actual standings task.
evidence.changedCase: Primary task changes to chronological match list.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All data being visible proves usable representation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match representation to changed user tasks.

interactions.title: Build standings grid
interactions.learnerAction: Build caption, justified columns, head, body groups, optional foot, rows, real header cells, data cells, tie data, and explicit missing-value text from league schema.

evidence.observable: DOM order, sections, roles, text, coordinates, and complete occupancy match data.
evidence.changedCase: Team row and metric column are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equal source cell counts prove grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Build parsed grid from current league schema.

interactions.title: Test standings grid mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, remove, reorder, and regroup teams and metrics; inspect parsed DOM, coordinates, roles, missing values, copy output, linear order, and occupancy.
interactions.artifactChange: Standings grid mutation evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Every coordinate remains covered once with intended role and data.
evidence.changedCase: Two metrics merge and one team changes division.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered alignment proves schema survived.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild rows from current data dimensions.

interactions.title: Build standings group spans
interactions.learnerAction: Build only necessary division rowspans and performance colspans from contiguous real groups, retaining repeated independent records and refusing decorative merges.
interactions.artifactChange: Standings spans and refusals are committed.

evidence.observable: Origins, extents, groups, width, occupancy, and linear meaning pass.
evidence.changedCase: Team moves division and metric group splits.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged labels always improve scan.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive spans from current group membership.

interactions.title: Test standings span mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, delete, and reorder teams and metrics inside and beside spans; recalculate, linearize, copy, and inspect every coordinate, hole, overlap, boundary, and shift.
interactions.artifactChange: Standings span mutation suite is stored.

evidence.observable: Complete grid and grouping remain exact under all geometry changes.
evidence.changedCase: Both group axes change together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Original span constants remain correct.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute geometry from current data groups.

interactions.title: Build standings header system
interactions.learnerAction: Build actual th and td roles, simple row and column scope, matching group scopes, and stable explicit ID/headers only where irregular multi-level context requires it.

evidence.observable: Every data cell receives exact team, metric, division, and performance-group context.
evidence.changedCase: Regular grouped metrics become irregular.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold styling or headers everywhere creates context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use simplest verified association per geometry.

interactions.title: Test every standings cell context
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder teams and metrics, change groups and spans, rename IDs, mutate references, navigate each cell, copy rows, and compare derived headers with expected context.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete standings header matrix is stored.

evidence.observable: Every token resolves and every cell receives complete relevant context only.
evidence.changedCase: All rows, columns, groups, and IDs mutate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Spot-checking first team proves table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify every cell after every geometry mutation.

interactions.title: Test responsive standings use
interactions.learnerAction: Use keyboard and touch table navigation, zoom, narrow, increase text, scroll grid locally, remove styles and pseudo-labels, copy, linearize, save, and reload.
interactions.artifactChange: Responsive and persistence evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Caption, full grid, focus, text, context, and page reflow remain usable.
evidence.changedCase: Longest team and metric names triple.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Stacked CSS labels preserve semantics automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve source relationships and test local scrolling.

interactions.title: Run adversarial standings mutation
interactions.learnerAction: Change representation task, caption, DOM order, teams, metrics, groups, missing data, spans, cell roles, scopes, IDs, references, zoom, width, save, and reload without broad rewrite.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete adversarial regression report is stored.

evidence.observable: All seven contracts survive minimal repairs, access paths, and persistence.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel league reverses every axis and group.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Original standings happy path is transfer evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Isolate first broken relationship and preserve others.

interactions.title: Defend standings design
interactions.learnerAction: Defend every representation, list, model, caption, grid section, cell role, empty value, span, scope, ID, header reference, cell context, responsive behavior, correction, and mutation.
interactions.artifactChange: Standings table and evidence-backed defense become lab checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every claim links data relationship, decision, geometry, context, access, and changed evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Stakeholder requests layout table, decorative spans, and CSS-generated labels.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Table element inventory substitutes for defense.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite weakest claim from data axes through cell context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed retrieval record demonstrates all seven concepts across unseen lists, models, captions, grids, spans, header contexts, and responsive traces without earlier artifacts.
activityDesigns.startingState: Unseen content briefs, candidate representations, table DOMs, grid maps, span diagrams, association matrices, and narrow-width traces are provided without notes.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner retrieves all seven concepts, corrects misconceptions causally, and transfers them after delay.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names missed concept and observable only after commitment and never supplies final table.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Optional retrieval hints point to one layer or smaller parallel cell.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss routes to concept-sized correction and different changed item.

interactions.title: Retrieve list relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Choose ordered, unordered, or term-description lists for unseen one-dimensional content and reject artificial row-column treatment.

evidence.observable: Sequence, grouping, and term relationships remain correct.
evidence.changedCase: Ranked content becomes unordered resources.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated items always fit table rows.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify one-dimensional relationship.

interactions.title: Predict table DOM recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict parsed and conforming relationships for changed caption, row-group, row, header, and data-cell parents and order.

evidence.observable: Predicted DOM and author rules match exact table contexts.
evidence.changedCase: Caption and row move to invalid parents.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source indentation predicts parsed table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply permitted contexts and parser behavior.

interactions.title: Retrieve table and control identification
interactions.learnerAction: Map page heading, table caption, orientation explanation, and interactive control labels for two unseen similar tables.

evidence.observable: Each table and control remains visible, distinct, and programmatically identified.
evidence.changedCase: Second caption becomes similar and filter moves.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One page heading identifies all tables.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name each table and control at own boundary.

interactions.title: Retrieve representation boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Choose table, list, definition list, prose, cards, or CSS layout for unseen multidimensional comparisons, sequences, facts, and page regions.

evidence.observable: Table appears only when intersecting axes are needed for tasks.
evidence.changedCase: Comparison collapses to one-axis feed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Desired grid visual chooses table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test relationship and information task.

interactions.title: Correct layout and over-table misuse
interactions.learnerAction: Correct page-layout table, one-dimensional card table, merged separate datasets, missing caption, and unnecessary table retained after task change.

evidence.observable: Alternatives and remaining tables support real meaning and tasks.
evidence.changedCase: Dataset gains second axis after correction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Presentation role or caption repairs every misuse.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reclassify underlying data relationship.

interactions.title: Predict unseen grid formation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict child order, sections, row and column counts, cell roles, text, coordinates, empty meanings, holes, overlaps, and linear order in changed table DOM.

evidence.observable: Predicted parsed grid covers every coordinate once with intended data.
evidence.changedCase: Row and column are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source cell count predicts grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Form grid from parsed rows and cells.

interactions.title: Correct structural table faults
interactions.learnerAction: Correct invalid child order, section misuse, styled data headers, spacing cells, implicit missing values, row-width mismatch, holes, and overlaps.

evidence.observable: DOM, roles, text, coordinates, occupancy, copy, and linearization pass.
evidence.changedCase: Body group and metric schema change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual alignment repairs structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild current data schema and grid.

interactions.title: Predict changed span geometry
interactions.learnerAction: Predict span necessity, origin, width, height, covered coordinates, group boundaries, grid width, holes, overlaps, and shifts for unseen rowspan and colspan mutations.

evidence.observable: Only real groups span and grid remains complete.
evidence.changedCase: Both group axes gain and lose members.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged box appearance defines geometry.
interactions.correctionRoute: Calculate every coordinate from current groups.

interactions.title: Correct decorative and stale spans
interactions.learnerAction: Correct decorative merges, invalid values, cross-group spans, stale constants, overlap, holes, and downstream shifts after changed rows and columns.

evidence.observable: Grouping, coordinates, occupancy, and linearized meaning pass.
evidence.changedCase: Rows reorder and groups split.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adjust span until rendering aligns.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute from current data relationship.

interactions.title: Correct header context failures
interactions.learnerAction: Correct styled td headers, th data, guessed scope, wrong group scope, duplicate IDs, dangling and unrelated headers tokens, missing contexts, and CSS-generated label contradictions.

evidence.observable: Every data cell receives exact complete relevant context using simplest valid mechanism.
evidence.changedCase: Regular table becomes irregular and IDs change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Add scope or headers everywhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive every association from actual geometry.

interactions.title: Explain delayed table model
interactions.learnerAction: Explain all seven concepts, dependencies, confusions, alternatives, DOM, geometry, contexts, responsive use, corrections, and unseen transfer without notes.
interactions.artifactChange: Delayed retrieval record becomes review checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Explanation links data axes, structure, grid, geometry, header context, access, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: New civic dataset combines irregular grouped axes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Table vocabulary substitutes for mental model.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild weakest concept from data to cell context.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently completed emergency-supply table demonstrates all seven concepts through answers, code, causal debugging, persistence, changed transfer, and defense.
activityDesigns.startingState: Unseen supply dataset, lookup tasks, alternative content, grouping facts, missing-data rules, responsive requirements, deterministic faults, and empty workspace are provided.
activityDesigns.completionState: Candidate independently demonstrates all seven concepts under original and changed conditions without instructional help.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Assessment feedback records only pass or failed requirement and observable; no canonical table, grid, span, context, or repair is disclosed.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No instructional hints, staged clues, example answers, or code completion are available.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failed item closes and records evidence gap for later relearning plus different parallel form.

interactions.title: Classify supply lists
interactions.learnerAction: Choose ordered, unordered, or term-description lists for unseen one-dimensional supply rules and reject artificial table axes.

evidence.observable: Sequence, grouping, and term relationships remain correct.
evidence.changedCase: Ranked steps become unordered resources.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated content belongs in rows.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record list-relationship evidence gap.

interactions.title: Debug supply table DOM
interactions.learnerAction: Repair invalid caption, row-group, row, header, and data-cell parentage and order in parsed unseen source.

evidence.observable: Parsed DOM and author relationships pass after source mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Footer and caption move again.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browser display proves valid source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record content-model evidence gap.

interactions.title: Code supply identification
interactions.learnerAction: Code visible page heading, concise table caption, bounded orientation text, and labels for any controls across two similar supply tables.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent table and control identification is committed.

evidence.observable: Each table and control remains visible, distinct, and programmatically named.
evidence.changedCase: Second table title becomes similar and control moves.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One page heading names all tables.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record table-identification evidence gap.

interactions.title: Choose supply representations
interactions.learnerAction: Choose table, list, definition list, prose, cards, or CSS layout for unseen supply comparisons, sequences, facts, and page regions; defend user tasks.

evidence.observable: Only intersecting row-column relationships use table.
evidence.changedCase: Comparison collapses to one-axis alert list.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Desired visual grid selects table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record table-purpose evidence gap.

interactions.title: Code supply grid
interactions.learnerAction: Code caption, justified columns and row groups, rows, actual header and data cells, explicit missing values, and optional summary from unseen supply schema.

evidence.observable: DOM order, sections, roles, text, coordinates, and occupancy match data.
evidence.changedCase: Location row and item column are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equal source cells prove grid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record grid-structure evidence gap.

interactions.title: Debug supply grid mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Repair row-group misuse, header/data confusion, spacing cells, implicit missing data, row-width mismatch, holes, overlaps, and parser repairs after row and column changes.

evidence.observable: Parsed grid, text, occupancy, copy, and linearization pass after mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Two columns merge and body groups change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual alignment repairs schema.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record grid-mutation evidence gap.

interactions.title: Derive supply span geometry
interactions.learnerAction: Derive necessary and refused rowspans and colspans, origins, extents, group boundaries, grid width, occupancy, and mutation costs from supply groups.

evidence.observable: Only real groups span and complete grid remains exact.
evidence.changedCase: Rows and columns move between groups.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged appearance defines span need.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record span-geometry evidence gap.

interactions.title: Debug supply spans
interactions.learnerAction: Repair decorative, invalid, stale, cross-group, overlap, hole, and downstream-shift spans after inserting and deleting grouped rows and columns.

evidence.observable: Origins, extents, groups, occupancy, and linear meaning pass.
evidence.changedCase: Both table axes regroup after a new supply category is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adjust values until boxes align.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record span-mutation evidence gap.

interactions.title: Design supply header contexts
interactions.learnerAction: Choose actual th and td roles, simple scope, matching group scope, or explicit unique ID/headers for every simple, grouped, and irregular supply relationship.

evidence.observable: Every data cell has simplest complete relevant context.
evidence.changedCase: Regular group becomes irregular multi-level header.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Add scope or headers everywhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record header-strategy evidence gap.

interactions.title: Debug every-cell supply context
interactions.learnerAction: Repair styled td headers, th data, wrong scopes, mismatched group spans, duplicate IDs, dangling and unrelated references, missing contexts, and CSS-label contradictions; inspect every cell.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent header repairs and context matrix are committed.

evidence.observable: All references resolve and every cell receives exact intended headers only.
evidence.changedCase: Rows, columns, groups, spans, and IDs change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First-row spot check proves table.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record per-cell-context evidence gap.

interactions.title: Test responsive supply table
interactions.learnerAction: Code bounded local table scrolling if needed, then navigate by keyboard and touch, zoom, narrow, increase text, remove styles and pseudo-labels, copy, linearize, save, and reload.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent responsive and persistence evidence is committed.

evidence.observable: Caption, full grid, focus, content, context, page reflow, and persistence remain usable.
evidence.changedCase: Longest labels triple and viewport narrows.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Stacked CSS labels or desktop screenshot prove access.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record responsive-table evidence gap.

interactions.title: Defend data-table mastery
interactions.learnerAction: Defend every representation, list, model, caption, grid, cell role, empty value, span, scope, ID, reference, cell context, responsive choice, repair, persistence result, and transfer without help.
interactions.artifactChange: Supply table and defense become independent assessment record.

evidence.observable: Every claim links data relationship, decision or refusal, grid, geometry, context, access, and mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel dataset requests layout table, decorative spans, and generated labels.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Table vocabulary substitutes for mastery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record weakest claim for relearning and parallel form.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, responsive, assistive-technology, and duplication reviews are not yet complete.

Learner-facing copy, fictional datasets and grid fixtures, header-association simulator, canonical answers, executable checks, browser and assistive-technology verification, and learner pilots remain unauthored.
